div1.entryFree: Latch, vb. to catch, to fall in with: it (my eye) no form delivers to the heart of bird, of flower, or shape, which it doth l. Sonn. 113, 6. but hast thou yet --ed the Athenian's eyes with the love-juice? Mids. III, 2, 36 (according to some, == anointed, smeared over, for which meaning of the word no other instance has been pointed out). I have words that would be howled out in the desert air, where hearing should not l. them, Mcb. IV, 3, 195. In Lr. II, 1, 54 Ff latched mine arm, Qq launcht.
